title: Frontend framework security upgrade and permanent dependency-admission policy
objective: Patch the vulnerable Next/React tree and make the owner's dependency-admission rule permanent, documented, and programmatically enforced (owner directive 2026-07-20, P0). REQUIRED EXACT TARGET: next==15.5.20, react==19.1.2, react-dom==19.1.2, eslint-config-next==15.5.20, package override postcss==8.5.10. HARD PROHIBITIONS: do NOT upgrade to Next 16; do NOT use a canary; do NOT run `npm audit fix --force`; do NOT accept an automated major downgrade or unrelated dependency rewrite. The producer MUST record exact publication dates + official registry/advisory evidence for every directly changed or overridden package and verify each ordinary changed package is >= 7 complete days old before committing; if registry/advisory state has changed, STOP and report - no silent substitution. The named targets already exceed seven days as of this directive (no emergency exception needed).
business_reason: The current Next/React tree carries known advisories; this P0 patches them to the exact reviewed target AND makes the owner's dependency-admission policy (advisory-free tree, 7-day release-age, exact pins, continuous audit) permanent and machine-enforced so future lockfile changes cannot silently reintroduce vulnerable or too-new packages (owner directive 2026-07-20).
inputs: Owner directive 2026-07-20 M0-T019 contract (verbatim requirements below)
The shared dependency-security policy concepts co-defined with M0-T018 (age/audit rules apply to both ecosystems)
outputs: apps/web/package.json + intentionally regenerated apps/web/package-lock.json at the exact target (next/react/react-dom/eslint-config-next) with "overrides": { "postcss": "8.5.10" }
CI npm tooling pinned to a reviewed npm 11 release supporting min-release-age (npm 11.18.0 is an eligible >7-day target as of this directive)
Project npm configuration: min-release-age=7, save-exact=true, normal integrity/lockfile verification retained

A BLOCKING full dependency audit (incl. dev deps): `npm audit --audit-level=low` AND JSON-output inspection requiring total vulnerabilities == 0; a warning-only audit step is NOT acceptable; npm ci stays deterministic (npm ci --no-audit permitted ONLY if followed by an explicit blocking audit)
A scheduled audit workflow (future advisories may affect an already-merged lockfile)
docs/DEPENDENCY_SECURITY_POLICY.md (permanent policy, full content per the owner contract)
A concise permanent dependency-security rule added to CLAUDE.md so future orchestrators must read it
project-control/reports/M0-T019-producer-report.md (AOS section-6 packet with per-package publication-date evidence and npm audit zero-findings JSON evidence)
allowed_paths: apps/web/.npmrc (or the repo npm config location) for min-release-age/save-exact
.github/workflows/** (npm tooling pin, blocking audit step, scheduled audit; existing jobs stay green)
CLAUDE.md (append the concise permanent dependency-security rule ONLY)

apps/web/scripts/dependency_age_gate.mjs - deterministic, fail-closed committed-lockfile release-age gate + continuous npm@11.18.0 CLI tooling advisory verification (owner policy-enforcement amendment 2026-07-20; Node ESM, node-builtins only, no npm deps)
apps/web/scripts/tests/** - the age-gate's deterministic positive / boundary (604800 pass, 604799 fail) / fail-closed unit tests
forbidden_paths: Next 16, canaries, `npm audit fix --force`, automated major downgrades, unrelated dependency rewrites

apps/web/src/** application/component/behavior changes (this is a dependency + policy task; a build/type fix strictly required by the framework bump must be minimal and disclosed, and must not change UI behavior or weaken tests/TS settings)
No weakening of tests or TypeScript settings; no warning-only audit; no advisory waiver by an agent
.claude/** (CLAUDE.md is a repo-root file, allowed; .claude/ tooling is not)
acceptance_scenarios: FE-S1 exact target: package.json + regenerated lockfile carry next==15.5.20, react==19.1.2, react-dom==19.1.2, eslint-config-next==15.5.20, and postcss override ==8.5.10; save-exact respected; no Next 16 / canary anywhere
FE-S2 audit zero (BLOCKING): `npm audit --audit-level=low` AND JSON total == 0; the audit step fails the build on any finding; dev deps included
FE-S3 deterministic install: npm ci reproduces the committed lockfile; integrity/lockfile verification intact; any --no-audit is followed by an explicit blocking audit
FE-S4 npm tooling + config: CI uses the pinned npm 11.18.0 (>7 days); min-release-age=7 and save-exact=true configured and effective
FE-S5 release-age: every ordinary changed/overridden package is >= 7 complete days old; per-package publication dates + official source URLs recorded in the report
FE-S6 full regression: npm ci; npm audit zero; lint pass; typecheck pass; complete Vitest suite pass; production Next build pass; existing Playwright journeys pass; secret scan pass
FE-S7 scheduled audit: a scheduled workflow re-audits the tree; failure path proven visible/actionable

FE-S8 permanent policy: docs/DEPENDENCY_SECURITY_POLICY.md contains the full owner-specified rule set (advisory-free tree across runtime/dev/build/audit-tooling; 7-day age; exact pins + lockfile integrity; audits on every change + schedule; no agent waiver; post-merge advisory reopens security work + blocks deploy; new-package provenance review of names/maintainers/lifecycle-scripts/registry-origin/publication-date/ownership-changes; prefer existing deps/stdlib over new packages) AND the emergency exception (age-requirement ONLY, never an advisory affecting the installed version, owner-authorized only, full record fields, auto-expiry at 7 days, no wildcard/org-wide/permanent/undocumented exceptions); CLAUDE.md carries the concise permanent rule pointer

FE-S9 committed-lockfile release-age gate (BLOCKING, machine-enforced, fail-closed) - the gap this amendment closes: .npmrc min-release-age is RESOLVER-TIME only and does NOT gate the committed lock that `npm ci` installs, so a hand-edited lockfile could smuggle a <7-day package past npm ci + npm audit. apps/web/scripts/dependency_age_gate.mjs must parse the ENTIRE committed apps/web/package-lock.json, enumerate every unique registry package name+version (direct, transitive, dev, test, build, optional, scoped, platform-specific), validate each entry resolves to registry.npmjs.org and carries an integrity that MATCHES the official registry dist.integrity for that version, obtain the official publication timestamp, and require age >= EXACTLY 604800 seconds against an authoritative current UTC (the npm registry Date header; injectable for tests). Boundary: 604800s PASSES, 604799s FAILS (full-second arithmetic, no day rounding). FAILS CLOSED (non-zero exit, package marked FAIL, never skipped) on registry outage/network error, missing/malformed publication timestamp, malformed lock entry, missing integrity, integrity mismatch, unexpected resolved host, or any unverifiable/ambiguous package. No allowlist / suppression / --ignore / exception path in the tool. Runs in required PR+push CI and in the scheduled npm-audit workflow. .npmrc min-release-age=7 is retained as defense-in-depth for regeneration.

FE-S10 exact direct pins: every direct dependency AND devDependency in apps/web/package.json is an EXACT version (no ^ or ~), equal to the version already resolved at the package-lock root. Converting the existing ranges introduces ZERO package-resolution change - the regenerated lock's resolved versions + integrity hashes are byte-for-byte the same set (proof: regenerate via the authorized npm 11.18.0 path and diff resolved versions; if ANY resolved version would change, STOP and report before proceeding). save-exact=true is retained for future adds.

FE-S11 npm CLI tooling continuous advisory verification (BLOCKING, fail-closed): the exact pinned npm@11.18.0 CLI tooling is checked for advisories affecting that version on every relevant CI run and in the scheduled npm-audit workflow (against the official advisory source); ANY advisory affecting npm@11.18.0 fails the run. No suppression/allowlist. This gives the dependency-management/audit tooling the same continuous machine advisory coverage the application tree has (parallels M0-T020's Python tooling-lock pip-audit). docs/DEPENDENCY_SECURITY_POLICY.md must accurately DISTINGUISH the four layers: (a) .npmrc resolver-time filtering, (b) independent committed-lockfile age verification in CI, (c) application-lock advisory auditing (npm audit), (d) npm CLI tooling advisory verification.
risks: P0 SEQUENCING (owner 2026-07-20): dispatched only AFTER M0-T018 is accepted; accepted BEFORE M2-T013/M2-T014 dispatch. Producer (frontend-engineer) cannot self-approve; G3 code-reviewer + G5 security-reviewer, both != producer.
TARGET VERSIONS PROVISIONAL on re-verification of publication timestamps + advisories at implementation; STOP and report if changed.
The framework bump may surface a minimal required build/type fix; it must not change UI behavior or weaken tests/TS - disclose any such touch line-by-line.

STOP CONDITIONS: no advisory-free compatible tree exists; a required fix is <7 days old needing an owner exception; any age/audit check skipped/downgraded/warning-only/allowlisted; tests require weakening; a forbidden path is needed; production credentials/deployment approval required.
Execution: GitHub Actions CI is authoritative for the web suites (thin-client policy: no local node_modules on the owner PC); local npm prohibited; disk-safe.

POLICY-ENFORCEMENT AMENDMENT (owner 2026-07-20, after the first independent review of task PR #64): the initial implementation enforced release-age only at lock RESOLUTION (.npmrc min-release-age), leaving the committed lock that `npm ci` installs ungated - a BLOCKING gap for a policy meant to stop every future lockfile change from admitting a too-new package. Acceptance now additionally requires FE-S9 (deterministic fail-closed committed-lockfile age gate apps/web/scripts/dependency_age_gate.mjs + tests), FE-S10 (exact direct/dev pins with zero resolution change), and FE-S11 (continuous npm@11.18.0 tooling advisory verification), all wired into CI + the scheduled npm-audit workflow. New allowed path: apps/web/scripts/**. Task PR #64 stays OPEN, receives new commits, and gets a FRESH G3/G4 + G5 review bound to the new head. NOT accepted; no checkpoint; no hold released; M0-T020 unchanged.
